# App Automaton

> An open-source workshop for engineering with coding agents.
> Portable SKILLs, stage-gated runtimes, and a quiet streak of pure-MLX work for Apple Silicon. Made for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, and OpenCode.

App Automaton is the open-source workshop of AppCubic. Everything public sorts onto four shelves: SKILLs that teach agents workflows, harnesses that keep them honest, models that run on the laptop's own silicon, and a creative harness that points the same method at club music. SKILLs are portable SKILL.md packs with three layers of disclosure (frontmatter the agent always sees, a workflow body loaded on invocation, references pulled on demand), and the same folder works unchanged under Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, and OpenCode.

## SKILLs for coding agents

- [agent-designer](https://github.com/appautomaton/agent-designer): the skills workspace. Issue-driven workflows, an MCP tool catalog, and bridge skills for agent-to-agent delegation with session continuity. [Deep dive](https://appautomaton.github.io/agent-designer/).
- [document-SKILLs](https://github.com/appautomaton/document-SKILLs): docx, xlsx, pptx, and pdf. Extraction, forms, formulas, and tracked changes. Runs on uv with PEP 723, no virtualenv.
- [presentation](https://github.com/appautomaton/presentation): consulting-quality decks, from strategy storyboarding to pixel-perfect PDF or editable PPTX.
- [webmaton](https://github.com/appautomaton/webmaton): grounded web research. Citations, deterministic HTML-to-Markdown, and persistent sessions over Playwright, nodriver, and Chrome DevTools.
- [playwright-skill](https://github.com/appautomaton/playwright-skill): browser automation that also runs on Android via a Termux launcher patch and headless Chromium.
- [latex-arxiv-SKILL](https://appautomaton.github.io/latex-arxiv-SKILL/): an agentic harness, as a portable Agent Skill, that turns a topic into an arXiv-ready ML review paper. Gated literature discovery, an issue-by-issue writing loop, every citation verified, compiled to a two-column IEEEtran PDF.

## Harnesses, runtimes, and tools

- [automaton](https://github.com/appautomaton/automaton): a stage-gated harness. Frame, plan, review, execute, verify, resume. Installs as plain markdown.
- [automux](https://github.com/appautomaton/automux): multi-agent orchestration in tmux or kitty, coordinating through files across parallel git worktrees.
- [openclaw-monorepo](https://github.com/appautomaton/openclaw-monorepo): a repo-local OpenClaw workspace with JSON5 config, plugins, and Docker sandboxes.
- [markmaton](https://github.com/appautomaton/markmaton): HTML to Markdown for agent pipelines. A Go engine wrapped in a Python CLI and API, on PyPI.
- [docker-for-apple-container](https://appautomaton.github.io/docker-for-apple-container/): a stateless `docker` shim over Apple's native `container` CLI on macOS, with no Docker Desktop.

## On-device, in pure MLX

- [mlx-speech](https://github.com/appautomaton/mlx-speech): speech synthesis, voice cloning, dialogue, sound effects, and recognition, MLX-native on the Apple GPU.
- [tnt-asr](https://github.com/appautomaton/tnt-asr): a terminal voice-to-text TUI. Qwen3-ASR transcribes in about a second, fully local.
- [ltx-video-mlx](https://github.com/appautomaton/ltx-video-mlx): text- and image-to-video with synchronized audio on LTX-2.3 22B. On-device LoRA fine-tuning.
- [mlx-cv](https://github.com/appautomaton/mlx-cv): MLX-native computer vision. Detection, segmentation, and open-vocabulary grounding with SAM 3 and LocateAnything.
- [mlx-spatial](https://github.com/appautomaton/mlx-spatial): 3D and spatial inference on device with SAM 3D Objects, TRELLIS.2, WorldMirror, and MapAnything.

## Creative harnesses

- [setloom](https://github.com/appautomaton/setloom): producer-first co-production for club music. Musical intent into editable tracks, stems, renders, and listening notes, with the human keeping the taste gate.

## Links

- [GitHub org](https://github.com/appautomaton): every public repo.
- [AppCubic](https://appcubic.com): the applied-AI venture studio behind App Automaton.
